steps.title: Apply the Zero Product Property
steps.content: The equation $(x-16)(x-12)(x+7)(x+13)=0$ is a product of four factors equal to zero. By the zero product property, this product equals zero if and only if at least one factor equals zero. Set each factor equal to zero:

steps.title: Solve each linear equation
steps.content:
$x - 16 = 0$ gives $x = 16$
$x - 12 = 0$ gives $x = 12$
$x + 7 = 0$ gives $x = -7$
$x + 13 = 0$ gives $x = -13$

The positive solutions are $x = 16$ and $x = 12$. Either 16 or 12 is a valid answer.
